name: EUR/USD
description: EUR/USD is the most traded currency pair in the world, representing the exchange rate between the Euro and the US Dollar. It accounts for approximately 24% of all daily Forex trading volume.
bestSession: London and New York overlap (13:00-16:00 GMT)
keyFactors: Political events in EU and US

name: GBP/USD
description: GBP/USD, known as 'Cable', is one of the most popular Forex pairs for binary options trading. It offers strong volatility during London market hours and clear trend patterns.

name: USD/JPY
description: USD/JPY is a major currency pair that reflects the relationship between the US and Japanese economies. It tends to show strong trends during Asian and US trading sessions.
bestSession: Asian session (00:00-09:00 GMT) and US session

name: AUD/JPY
description: AUD/JPY is a popular cross pair for binary options, often used as a risk sentiment indicator. It offers clear trending movements and is active during Asian and Pacific trading hours.

name: BTC/USD
description: BTC/USD represents the price of Bitcoin in US Dollars. It is the most liquid cryptocurrency pair, offering 24/7 trading opportunities with high volatility and strong trend movements.
bestSession: 24/7 availability, highest volume during US hours

name: ETH/USD
description: ETH/USD tracks the value of Ethereum against the US Dollar. As the second-largest cryptocurrency, Ethereum offers excellent trading opportunities with strong daily volatility.
bestSession: 24/7 availability, volatile during US market hours

name: Gold (XAU/USD)
description: Gold (XAU/USD) is one of the most popular commodities for binary options trading. It serves as a safe-haven asset and tends to rally during economic uncertainty and inflation.

name: Oil (WTI)
description: WTI Crude Oil is a highly traded commodity that offers binary options traders excellent volatility and trend-based trading opportunities, especially around OPEC decisions and inventory reports.

name: EUR/USD OTC
description: EUR/USD OTC is an over-the-counter version of the popular EUR/USD pair, available for trading 24/7 including weekends. OTC markets use synthetic pricing and offer continuous trading opportunities.
keyFactors: Different pattern behavior vs live markets

name: GBP/JPY
description: GBP/JPY, nicknamed 'The Beast', is known for its high volatility and large daily ranges. It is favored by experienced binary options traders who thrive on fast-moving markets.

name: NZD/USD
description: NZD/USD, known as the 'Kiwi', is a popular commodity-linked currency pair. It is heavily influenced by dairy prices, trade relations with China, and RBNZ monetary policy decisions.
bestSession: Asian-Pacific session (21:00-06:00 GMT) and US session

name: USD/CAD
description: USD/CAD, called the 'Loonie', reflects the relationship between the US and Canadian economies. It is strongly correlated with oil prices since Canada is a major crude oil exporter.

name: EUR/GBP
description: EUR/GBP is a popular European cross pair that represents the exchange rate between the Eurozone and the UK. It tends to have lower volatility than major USD pairs, making it suitable for range-trading strategies.
keyFactors: ECB vs Bank of England policy divergence

name: EUR/JPY
description: EUR/JPY is a widely traded cross pair that combines European economic factors with Japanese monetary policy. It offers good volatility and trending characteristics, popular for both scalping and swing trading.

name: AUD/USD
description: AUD/USD, called the 'Aussie', is a major commodity currency pair. It is closely tied to iron ore and gold prices, Chinese demand, and Reserve Bank of Australia interest rate decisions.
bestSession: Asian-Pacific session (00:00-06:00 GMT) and US session

name: USD/CHF
description: USD/CHF, known as the 'Swissie', pairs the US Dollar with the Swiss Franc safe-haven currency. It often moves inversely to EUR/USD and is popular during periods of market uncertainty.
bestSession: London and New York sessions (08:00-17:00 GMT)

name: NASDAQ 100
description: The NASDAQ 100 index tracks the top 100 non-financial companies listed on NASDAQ, including tech giants like Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon. It offers binary options trading on overall tech sector sentiment.
